subject: [PATCH v5 08/22] x86/virt/seamldr: Allocate and populate a module update request

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau
reviews.summary: Raised concerns about alignment and offset calculations in the code. Suggested adding a comment to clarify the relationship between block->offset_of_module and struct tdx_blob.

2026-03-17.patch_summary: Reviewer Kiryl Shutsemau pointed out potential issues with alignment and offset calculations in the code.

reviews.author: Chao Gao
reviews.summary:
reviewer pointed out that PAGE_SIZE is not guaranteed to be 4096 and suggested two options to address the concern: adding a WARN_ON_ONCE or handling it as in the code above

Reviewer pointed out that the code assumes @sig is page-aligned, but it's actually 4k aligned in VA; they suggested adding a comment to clarify that block->offset_of_module is relative to the start of struct tdx_blob and not blob->data
reviews.raw_body:
Dan's concern was that PAGE_SIZE is not guaranteed to be 4096.

I agree that PAGE_SIZE is 4K on x86 today. But to address that concern, I saw
two options:

  1. Add WARN_ON_ONCE(PAGE_SIZE != SZ_4K), or
  2. Handle it as in the code above.

I didn't find existing code using option 1 in x86, so I chose option 2.

---

@sig is 4KB aligned.

<snip>

2026-03-18.patch_summary: This patch corrects an issue in the x86/virt/seamldr module where it incorrectly handles memory alignment for a signature (@sig) that is not 4k aligned. The fix involves adjusting the pointer to the next 4k boundary when handling such cases. Additionally, the patch adds a comment to clarify how block->offset_of_module is calculated relative to the start of the struct tdx_blob.

reviews.author: Chao Gao
reviews.summary: Reviewer pointed out that @sig being 4k aligned does not necessarily imply page size alignment, and suggested adding a comment to clarify that block->offset_of_module is relative to the start of struct tdx_blob, not blob->data.
reviews.raw_body:
Sure. How about:

		/*
		 * @sig is 4KB-aligned, but that does not imply PAGE_SIZE
		 * alignment when PAGE_SIZE != SZ_4K. Always include the
		 * in-page offset.
		 */

2026-03-19.patch_summary: This patch corrects an issue in the x86/virt/seamldr module where it incorrectly handles memory alignment for a signature (@sig) that is not 4k aligned. The fix involves adjusting the pointer to the next 4k boundary when handling such cases. Additionally, the patch adds a comment to clarify how block->offset_of_module is calculated relative to the start of the struct tdx_blob.
